title: Lasso Peptides—A New Weapon Against Superbugs
objective: Review the structural, synthetic, and antibacterial properties of lasso peptides as potential antimicrobial agents against multi-drug-resistant bacteria (superbugs)
problem_statement: The emergence of multi-drug-resistant (MDR) bacteria represents one of the greatest challenges for modern medicine. Current antibiotics have become ineffective due to rapid resistance development. Lasso peptides represent a promising new class of RiPPs with unique structural properties that could bind to new molecular targets and overcome existing resistance mechanisms

lasso_peptide_definition.name: Lasso Peptides (Lariat Peptides)
lasso_peptide_definition.type: Ribosomally synthesized and post-translationally modified peptides (RiPPs)
lasso_peptide_definition.key_structural_feature: C-terminal tail trapped in a macrolactam ring forming a slip knot-like shape
lasso_peptide_definition.structure: Structurally constrained knotted fold resembling a lasso
lasso_peptide_definition.ring_formation: Isopeptide bond between N-terminal amino group and β- or γ-carboxyl group of Asp/Glu

class_II.description: Most common class without multiple disulfide bridges

class_V.description: Double-lariat structures with additional isopeptide bond

mechanisms: Binding to unique molecular targets of multi-drug-resistant bacteria

physical_properties.thermal_stability: Remarkable thermal stability in physiological environment
physical_properties.proteolytic_stability: Remarkable proteolytic stability in physiological environment
physical_properties.conformational_freedom: Constrained due to lasso structure (unlike typical peptides)

biosynthesis_characteristics.expression: Expression of a cluster of genes encoding
biosynthesis_characteristics.formation_mechanism: Post-translational processing leading to ring closure and lasso structure formation

synthetic_challenges.major_challenge: Recreating lasso peptide synthesis using modern organic chemistry is a virtually unsolved challenge
synthetic_challenges.reason: Atypical rigid structural element (macrolactam ring with threaded tail) makes chemical synthesis extremely difficult
synthetic_challenges.advantage_of_biological_synthesis: Biosynthetic systems solve this problem naturally

advantages: Can be conjugated with other bioactive molecules
Bind to new molecular targets unlike conventional antibiotics

known_lasso_peptides_with_data.name: Lariocidin (LAR)
known_lasso_peptides_with_data.activity: Broad-spectrum lasso peptide antibiotic targeting bacterial ribosome

known_lasso_peptides_with_data.name: Lassomycin
known_lasso_peptides_with_data.activity: Targets microbial ClpP protease, antimycobacterial activity

known_lasso_peptides_with_data.name: Capistruin
known_lasso_peptides_with_data.activity: Active against Gram-negative bacteria, blocks RNA polymerase
